steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses joined by 'and.' The second clause has the plural subject 'its theoretical linguistic properties' and needs a finite main verb at the blank.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a participial or non-finite form: Participles cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause, so this leaves the clause without a verb. ✗
  • Choice B — reflect: 'Reflect' is a finite verb that agrees with the plural subject 'properties' and completes the independent clause. ✓
  • Choice C — another non-finite form: Same problem as A — no main verb for the clause. ✗
  • Choice D — a relative-clause construction: Introducing a relative clause here leaves the independent clause without its own main verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
